steps: Export your CRM data and immediately strip it down to aggregate counts and cohorts only — no names, no emails, no addresses, no gift amounts tied to a person.

Save that stripped, aggregate-only file in a folder on your own machine. This job never goes near a cloud routine — a routine runs on someone else's servers, and it can only be trusted with what you're willing to upload.
Set up a local scheduled task on a station computer, not a cloud routine, to run the aggregation on a monthly schedule.
Ask Claude Code to summarize only the aggregate counts and cohort trends into a plain-English brief — never the underlying export.
Read the draft brief yourself and confirm every number in it traces back to the aggregate file, not to anything more detailed.
Only after your review does the brief go to leadership.
verify: Open the aggregate file the brief was built from and search it for an @ sign or anything that looks like a name. If anything personally identifying survived the strip, stop and fix the export before this runs again.
